root_text.ud1.9:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā gayāyaṁ viharati gayāsīse.
root_text.ud1.9:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā jaṭilā sītāsu hemantikāsu rattīsu antaraṭṭhake himapātasamaye gayāyaṁ ummujjantipi nimujjantipi, ummujjanimujjampi karonti osiñcantipi, aggimpi juhanti:
root_text.ud1.9:2.1: Addasā kho bhagavā te sambahule jaṭile sītāsu hemantikāsu rattīsu antaraṭṭhake himapātasamaye gayāyaṁ ummujjantepi nimujjantepi ummujjanimujjampi karonte osiñcantepi aggimpi juhante:
root_text.ud1.9:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud1.9:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Gayā on Gayā Head.
translation_text.ud1.9:1.3: Now at that time—during the cold spell when the snow falls in the January winter—many dreadlocked ascetics plunged in and out of the Gayā river. Plunging and showering, they served the sacred flame, thinking:
translation_text.ud1.9:2.1: The Buddha saw them plunging in and out.
translation_text.ud1.9:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud1.9:4.2: no matter how many people bathe there.
translation_text.ud1.9:4.3: One who has truth and principle,
translation_text.ud1.9:4.4: they are pure, they are brahmins.”

variant_text.ud1.9:2.1: ummujjanimujjampi karonte → ummujjanimujjaṁ karontepi (bj, pts-vp-pli1, mr); ummujjanimmujjampi karonte (sya-all)
